runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain bounds.

This combines material enhancement with geometric expansion to maximize lateral heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance.

runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500 W/mK), and geometry changes are constrained by legality limits. The most effective remaining lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction from the spreader to the cooled boundaries. We will increase base_material.conductivity from 12.0 to 24.0 (2x step, within bounds) to enhance downstream thermal transport.

runs.llm_decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. However, the optimization history indicates diminishing returns from geometry changes and suggests shifting to material tuning. Since the heat spreader conductivity is at its upper bound (500.0), the next most effective lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve downstream heat extraction toward the side sinks.
